How's it going everybody? In this video I'm going to walk you through how to download and install Google Chrome on a Windows computer. I'm using Windows 10 and I'm going to use the built-in default browser edge to walk you through the process. If you're using a different version of Windows or a different web browser to watch this right now, your process may be a bit different. Below this video we're going to give you a button that opens a link to this download and install Google Chrome page. Now at some point Google may update how this page looks, but it should be pretty much the same once you open it up. You're looking for this section that says install Chrome on Windows. It says download the installation file. Go ahead and click on that download the installation file and when you click on it, it should take you to the install Google Chrome page to how to download it. Now they've changed the appearance of this page as well a few times, but normally you're looking for this blue button that says download Chrome. Once you click on download Chrome, I'm going to ask if you want to go to an agree to the terms of service. Make sure you read those and you understand. I always like to uncheck that box. It's just my personal preference. You can do what you want with it and then once you're ready go and hit this blue accept and install button. Now at the bottom we have this run save or cancel. I'm going to go ahead and choose save and it's going to save it. Now again a different browser is going to handle that download differently, but once you get it downloaded you want to go and open up and run it. We have this open file security warning. It's going to ask if you're sure you want to run it. Go and click run again. Do you want to allow this app to make changes to your device? Go and hit yes if it says Google setup or something like that. Now it's going to do kind of another download to make sure you get the latest version and then start installing Google Chrome run away. Now depending on how fast your computer is, how fast your internet is, it may take a bit longer. Now this is the initial setup page for Chrome. That's perfectly fine. Sometimes it looks a bit different. If you need to get to it later, you should create an icon on your desktop right there. Now we do recommend to use Google Chrome for all of this. Watching the videos, going through the tutorials, following along because that's what we tested everything in as Google Chrome and we know it works there. If you use a different browser, things may not work the same or as you expect. So you may end up having some issues. So make sure you download and install Google Chrome regardless of what operating system you're using. If you already have Google Chrome, you can just use that one. You don't have to download it again. Well that's it. I hope you enjoyed this video and I hope you enjoy the rest of them.
